full_prompt: Design the primary load-bearing airframe tube assembly (nose-cone to boat-tail, excluding motor case) of a student-built sounding rocket competing in the national aerospace student benchmark 2025 Roket Yarışması Yüksek İrtifa (high-altitude) category, targeting a nominal apogee of 15000 ft (4572 m) AGL with a maximum allowable apogee of 18000 ft (5486 m) AGL. The vehicle uses a student-researched and developed (SRAD) solid-propellant motor permitted in this class. Gross lift-off weight (GLOW) must not exceed the category cap of 30 kg wet. Minimum rail-exit velocity from a 5.2 m launch rail is 30 m/s. The airframe outer diameter is 152 mm (6 inch) with a total length of 2.8 m from nose tip to aft boat-tail. The airframe must be a multi-section tubular structure (nose cone + payload bay + recovery bay + motor mount bay + fin can) joined by shear-pin and coupler interfaces, fabricated from filament-wound glass-fiber reinforced composite (G10/FR4 or equivalent) with laminate Young's modulus 18 GPa (longitudinal), ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa, compressive strength 240 MPa, interlaminar shear strength 35 MPa, and density 1850 kg/m^3. Recovery is dual-event: drogue deploys at apogee, main parachute deploys at 450 m AGL, both fired by a commercial dual-redundant electronic altimeter chain (e.g. StratoLoggerCF + RRC3). Load cases to be verified: (LC1) peak motor thrust of 4000 N applied axially at the motor mount bulkhead with gravity and aerodynamic drag reactions distributed over the body; (LC2) maximum aerodynamic bending moment at max-Q corresponding to 2 degree angle of attack at Mach 0.9 producing a distributed side load equivalent to 1800 N applied at the aerodynamic center; (LC3) drogue deployment shock of 3000 N applied axially at the drogue attachment bulkhead; (LC4) main parachute opening shock of 4500 N applied axially at the main parachute eyebolt; (LC5) internal recovery-bay ejection pressure pulse of 50 kPa applied to the coupler and bulkhead interfaces. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) maximum von Mises (or Tsai-Wu composite failure index equivalent) stress must stay below ultimate divided by a factor of safety of 2.0 (so below 155 MPa in tension, below 120 MPa in compression, below 17.5 MPa interlaminar shear) for every load case; (R2) linear buckling eigenvalue load factor for the worst compressive case (LC1) must be at least 1.5 on the slenderest body tube section; (R3) maximum airframe deflection at the nose tip relative to the fin can must not exceed 15 mm under LC2; (R4) airframe-tube system dry mass must remain below 6.0 kg to meet the GLOW budget; (R5) fin-root shear stress under LC2 must stay below 17.5 MPa. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required for this structural qualification; the external aerodynamic pressure distribution has already been reduced to the equivalent side load in LC2. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly with each tube section as a separate solid body, composite layup definition (ply sequence, thickness per section), FEA report with stress/deflection per load case, linear-buckling report for LC1, and mass budget summary.
short_prompt:
Design the primary load-bearing airframe tube assembly (nose-cone through boat-tail, excluding motor case) of a student-built sounding rocket in the national aerospace student benchmark 2025 Roket Yarışması Yüksek İrtifa category. Target apogee 15000 ft AGL (max 18000 ft), GLOW <= 30 kg wet, rail-exit >= 30 m/s from a 5.2 m launch rail. SRAD solid motor permitted.

Envelope: 152 mm (6 in) OD x 2.8 m total, multi-section tubular airframe (nose, payload, recovery, motor mount, fin can) joined by shear-pin + coupler interfaces; filament-wound G10/FR4 composite. Dual-event recovery (drogue at apogee, main at 450 m AGL).

Factor regime: FoS 2.0 on ultimate (tensile 155 MPa / compressive 120 MPa / ILSS 17.5 MPa); linear buckling eigenvalue >= 1.5 on the worst compressive case; nose-tip deflection relative to fin can <= 15 mm under max-Q bending.

Load cases.
- Peak motor thrust 4000 N axial at the motor-mount bulkhead.
- Max-Q bending: equivalent 1800 N side load at the aerodynamic center (Mach 0.9, 2 deg AoA).
- Drogue deployment shock 3000 N axial at the drogue bulkhead.
- Main parachute opening shock 4500 N axial at the main eyebolt.
- Recovery-bay ejection pulse: 50 kPa internal on the coupler/bulkhead.

prompt.title: national aerospace student benchmark 2025 High-Altitude Rocket Airframe Tube Assembly
prompt.brief: Composite airframe tube assembly for 15000 ft apogee SRAD-propelled sounding rocket in national aerospace student benchmark Yüksek İrtifa class.

mission.recovery: dual_event: drogue at apogee + main at 450 m AGL

load_cases.name: max_Q_bending
load_cases.application: aerodynamic center side load at 2 deg AoA, Mach 0.9

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: layered shell (SHELL281 / S4R) for tubes; solid TET10 for bulkheads

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Motor combustion vibration and wind-induced flutter are screened out; only peak quasi-static thrust retained.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Short mission duration (< 60 s motor burn) and external aero heating below 80 C for this apogee class are not driving.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aero loads reduced to LC2 equivalent side load.
